input.content:
# Kapow Data Sources

Comprehensive reference for every data source in the kapow data pipeline.
Each section documents the URL, access method, file format, update cadence,
fetch command, and any manual steps required.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Annual Import Schedule](#annual-import-schedule)
- [Pipeline Ordering](#pipeline-ordering)
- [Foundation Data](#foundation-data)
  - [College Scorecard (NCES/IPEDS)](#college-scorecard-ncesipeds)
  - [College Scorecard Field of Study](#college-scorecard-field-of-study)
  - [Urban Institute Education Data Portal](#urban-institute-education-data-portal)
- [Rankings](#rankings)
  - [US News & World Report](#us-news--world-report)
  - [Forbes](#forbes)
  - [Niche](#niche)
  - [Times Higher Education](#times-higher-education)
  - [QS World University Rankings](#qs-world-university-rankings)
  - [Washington Monthly](#washington-monthly)
  - [WSJ/College Pulse](#wsjcollege-pulse)
  - [ARWU / Shanghai Ranking](#arwu--shanghai-ranking)
  - [Money Magazine](#money-magazine)
- [Athletics](#athletics)
  - [NCAA Membership Directory](#ncaa-membership-directory)
- [Geographic & Environmental](#geographic--environmental)
  - [Köppen Climate Classification](#köppen-climate-classification)
  - [ZIP Code Coordinates (GeoNames)](#zip-code-coordinates-geonames)
- [Political & Demographic](#political--demographic)
  - [FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean](#fivethirtyeight-partisan-lean)
- [Campus Safety](#campus-safety)
  - [Clery Act Crime Data](#clery-act-crime-data)
- [Institutional Metadata](#institutional-metadata)
  - [University Hex Colors](#university-hex-colors)
  - [Application Platform Membership](#application-platform-membership)

---

## Annual Import Schedule

Run sources in this order each year. The `run-all` command handles dependency
ordering automatically, but this calendar shows when new data becomes available.

| Month | Sources Available | Command |
|-------|-------------------|---------|
| **May–Jun** | College Scorecard + FOS, QS World, Money | `kapow import nces && kapow import nces-fos` |
| **Aug–Sep** | ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ | `kapow import rankings --publication=<slug>` |
| **Oct–Nov** | Times Higher Education, Crime/Clery | `kapow import rankings --publication=the && kapow import crime` |
| **Any time** | Köppen, ZIP coords, partisan lean, hex colors, app platforms | `kapow run-all --only=weather,zipcoords,misc` |
| **After each batch** | Normalizer + materialized view refresh | `kapow import normalizer` (automatic in `run-all`) |

---

## Pipeline Ordering

The `run-all` command executes importers in dependency order using topological
sort:

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

---

## Foundation Data

### College Scorecard (NCES/IPEDS)

The primary institutional data source. All other importers match against
institutions established by this import.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download (ZIP archive) |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual. Late April–May (Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024). October update adds FSA data. |
| **Expected by** | Jun 30 |
| **DB target** | `search_base.institution` core columns + `nces` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import nces
kapow import nces --file /path/to/Most-Recent-Cohorts-Institution.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- Institution identity: name, unit_id, OPE6/OPE8 IDs, city, state, coordinates
- Demographics, test scores, costs, admissions rates, enrollment
- 200+ columns mapped to JSONB keys

**Special handling:**
- OPE6/OPE8 deduplication: prefers lower unit_id when multiple campuses share an OPE
- Filters out non-degree-granting institutions
- This is Layer 0 — must run before any other importer

---

### College Scorecard Field of Study

CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) data from the same Scorecard
release.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ (FieldOfStudyData files) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download (ZIP archive) |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Update cadence** | Same as College Scorecard |
| **Expected by** | Jun 30 |
| **DB target** | `search_base.cip_data` + `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import nces-fos
```

**What it imports:**
- CIP codes and program names by institution
- Credential levels: 1=Certificate, 2=Associate, 3=Bachelor's, 4=Master's, 5=Doctoral, 6=Professional
- Earnings and debt data by program

---

### Urban Institute Education Data Portal

Aggregated API that provides cleaned, standardized access to IPEDS, Scorecard,
EADA, Crime, FSA, NACUBO, NHGIS, and PSEO data.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://educationdata.urban.org/ |
| **API docs** | https://educationdata.urban.org/documentation/ |
| **Access method** | REST API (JSON) — no authentication required |
| **File format** | JSON (paginated API responses) |
| **Update cadence** | Varies by dataset; generally follows original source release dates |
| **Expected by** | N/A — data appears after Urban processes upstream releases |

**API pattern:**

```
https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/ipeds/directory/{year}/
https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/scorecard/earnings/{year}/
```

**Datasets available through this API:**
- **IPEDS** — institutional characteristics, enrollment, completions, finance
- **College Scorecard** — earnings, debt, repayment
- **EADA** — Equity in Athletics Disclosure Act data
- **Campus Safety (Clery)** — crime statistics
- **FSA** — Federal Student Aid
- **NACUBO** — endowment data
- **NHGIS** — geographic/census data
- **PSEO** — Post-Secondary Employment Outcomes

**When to use:** The Urban Institute API is useful as a supplementary or
alternative source when the raw CSV downloads from Scorecard or IPEDS are
difficult to parse, or when you need pre-joined cross-dataset queries. The
primary kapow pipeline uses direct CSV downloads for the core Scorecard import
but may use this API for supplementary datasets (EADA, NACUBO, PSEO).

---

## Rankings

All ranking importers share common infrastructure:
- **Name matching**: Tier 1 (exact/normalized/alias) → Tier 2 (Jaro-Winkler fuzzy) → Tier 3 (LLM, optional)
- **Output**: `search_base.ranking` metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB
- **Dependency**: Requires NCES import first (Layer 1)

### US News & World Report

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings |
| **Access method** | GitHub CSV download (historical archive 1984–2024) |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late September |
| **Expected by** | Oct 15 |
| **Categories** | National Universities, Liberal Arts Colleges |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=usnews
kapow import rankings --publication=usnews --file /path/to/usnews.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- Numeric ranks and tier strings (e.g., "Tier 1", "51-75")
- Two separate ranking lists: national universities and liberal arts colleges
- Historical data back to 1984

**Manual steps:** For the current year's data (before it appears in the frishberg
archive), you may need to manually obtain the CSV from US News or scrape
`usnews.com/best-colleges/rankings/national-universities`.

---

### Forbes

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000` |
| **Access method** | JSON API (public, no authentication) |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August–early September |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=forbes
kapow import rankings --publication=forbes --year 2025
```

**API URL pattern:**

```
https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=organizationName,rank,state,uri
```

**What it imports:**
- Single combined list (not split by category)
- Rank, institution name, state

---

### Niche

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/` |
| **Access method** | Paginated JSON API (requires specific headers) |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=niche
```

**API URL pattern:**

```
https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college
```

**Special handling:**
- 109 pages of results
- **Required headers**: `User-Agent` (browser-like) + `Referer: https://www.niche.com/colleges/search/best-colleges/`
- **Rate limiting**: 200ms sleep between page requests
- Responses include IPEDS IDs, enabling direct matching without name resolution

---

### Times Higher Education

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` |
| **Alternative** | https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data |
| **Access method** | JSON API (behind page) or frishberg CSV |
| **File format** | JSON or CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, early–mid October |
| **Expected by** | Nov 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=the
kapow import rankings --publication=the --file /path/to/the-rankings.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit the THE rankings page in a browser
2. Open browser dev tools → Network tab
3. Filter for XHR/JSON requests to find the API endpoint
4. Download the JSON response
5. Provide via `--file` flag

Alternatively, use the frishberg CSV archive if available for the target year.

**What it imports:**
- Numeric ranks and tier bands (e.g., "201-250", "401-500")
- Name matching via aliases for institutions with non-standard names

---

### QS World University Rankings

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data |
| **Access method** | TSV/CSV download |
| **File format** | TSV (tab-separated) or CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, mid June |
| **Expected by** | Jul 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=qs
kapow import rankings --publication=qs --file /path/to/qs-world.tsv
```

**Special handling:**
- Handles tied ranks denoted by trailing `=` (e.g., "51=")
- Multiple years may be present in a single file
- Filter to US institutions only during import

---

### Washington Monthly

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://washingtonmonthly.com/rankings/` |
| **Access method** | JSON or web scrape |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August (published with Sep/Oct issue) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=washmonthly
```

**What it imports:**
- Category-based rankings: National Universities, Liberal Arts Colleges
- Matching by unit_id when available, name matching as fallback

**Manual steps:** Check current website structure — the data format may change
year to year. If automated download fails, use browser dev tools to capture the
JSON payload and provide via `--file`.

---

### WSJ/College Pulse

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | WSJ rankings page |
| **Access method** | JSON embedded in page source |
| **File format** | JSON (`{ colleges: [...] }` structure) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, early September |
| **Expected by** | Oct 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj --file /path/to/wsj.json
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit the WSJ college rankings page
2. View page source or use browser dev tools
3. Search for the JSON object containing `colleges` array
4. Save the JSON to a file
5. Import with `--file` flag

**What it imports:**
- Direct unit_id matching where available
- Name fallback for unmatched institutions

---

### ARWU / Shanghai Ranking

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` |
| **Access method** | HTML scrape or PDF download |
| **File format** | HTML table or PDF |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, mid August (consistently since 2003) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=arwu
kapow import rankings --publication=arwu --file /path/to/arwu.json
```

**Manual steps (if automated scrape fails):**
1. Visit `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}`
2. Option A: Use goquery to scrape the HTML table
3. Option B: Download PDF from `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`
4. Extract institution names and ranks into JSON/CSV
5. Import with `--file` flag

**What it imports:**
- Global ranks (top 500–1000 institutions)
- Filter to US institutions during import

---

### Money Magazine

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://money.com/best-colleges/` |
| **Access method** | HTML scrape |
| **File format** | HTML table with embedded data |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late June |
| **Expected by** | Jul 31 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=money
kapow import rankings --publication=money --file /path/to/money.json
```

**Manual steps (if automated scrape fails):**
1. Visit `money.com/best-colleges/`
2. Extract `tr[data-id]` rows from the HTML table
3. Parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` elements via goquery
4. Save as JSON/CSV and import with `--file`

---

## Athletics

### NCAA Membership Directory

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` |
| **Access method** | CSV export or Playwright browser automation |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, August (academic year start) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 30 |
| **DB target** | `institution.athletics` JSONB + `search_base.conference` |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import ncaa
kapow import ncaa --file /path/to/ncaa-directory.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit https://web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList
2. Export the full member list as CSV (or use Playwright-based scraper)
3. Import with `--file` flag if manual export

**What it imports:**
- Division (I, II, III) and subdivision (FBS, FCS for football)
- Conference membership
- 38 sports mapped to gender categories (men/women/coed)
- Institution name matching via Tier 1/2/3

---

## Geographic & Environmental

### Köppen Climate Classification

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | Public domain ASCII grid (Kottek et al. or Beck et al.) |
| **Access method** | Direct download |
| **File format** | ASCII text (space-delimited grid: lat, lon, classification) |
| **Update cadence** | Stable — updated approximately every 10 years |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.weather` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import weather
kapow import weather --file /path/to/koppen.txt
```

**What it imports:**
- Köppen-Geiger climate classification code for each institution's coordinates
- E.g., Cfa (humid subtropical), Dfb (warm-summer humid continental)

**Special handling:**
- Grid-based lookup: normalizes coordinates to 0.25°/0.75° grid
- Spiral search up to 3° for coastal institutions where the nearest grid cell is ocean
- Requires institution coordinates from NCES import

---

### ZIP Code Coordinates (GeoNames)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | GeoNames postal code data (public domain) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP code, latitude, longitude, municipality, state) |
| **Update cadence** | Stable — updated continuously but rarely changes |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `location.zip_coordinates` (PostGIS geography) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import zipcoords
kapow import zipcoords --file /path/to/zipcodes.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- ~42,000 US ZIP codes with latitude/longitude
- PostGIS geography points for spatial queries

**Special handling:**
- Layer 0 importer (no dependencies)
- Bulk insert via `COPY FROM` for performance

---

## Political & Demographic

### FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | FiveThirtyEight GitHub repository (CSV) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download from GitHub |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Post-election, every 2 years (typically March in odd years) |
| **Expected by** | Mar 31 (odd years, e.g., 2027, 2029) |
| **DB target** | `institution.misc` JSONB (`partisan_lean` key) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import misc --source=partisan
```

**What it imports:**
- Congressional-district-level partisan lean scores (D+/R+ values)
- Matched to institutions via PostGIS spatial join to congressional district boundaries

**Special handling:**
- Requires institution coordinates from NCES import
- Requires congressional district geometries in the database
- Score represents the district where the institution is physically located

---

## Campus Safety

### Clery Act Crime Data

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list |
| **Access method** | Direct download (XLS files, converted to CSV) |
| **File format** | XLS → CSV (requires LibreOffice for conversion) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual. Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **Expected by** | Nov 15 |
| **DB target** | `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import crime
kapow import crime --file /path/to/crime-oncampus.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list
2. Download the XLS files for the target year
3. Convert to CSV using LibreOffice:
   ```bash
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv oncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv noncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv vawa-oncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv vawa-noncampus.xls
   ```
4. Import the converted CSVs

**What it imports:**
- On-campus crime statistics (murder, robbery, burglary, etc.)
- Non-campus crime statistics
- VAWA categories (domestic violence, dating violence, stalking)
- Deduplicates by OPE8 ID (aggregates across branch campuses)

**Files required (4 CSVs per year):**

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `oncampus.csv` | On-campus crime counts |
| `noncampus.csv` | Non-campus crime counts |
| `vawa-oncampus.csv` | VAWA on-campus incidents |
| `vawa-noncampus.csv` | VAWA non-campus incidents |

---

## Institutional Metadata

### University Hex Colors

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/University-Hex-Colors |
| **Access method** | GitHub JSON download |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Community-maintained; check periodically |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.misc` JSONB (`hex_color` key) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import misc --source=hexcolors
kapow import misc --source=hexcolors --file /path/to/colors.json
```

**What it imports:**
- Primary and secondary hex color codes for universities
- Used for branding in the frontend UI

**Special handling:**
- Name matching via Tier 1/2 to link color entries to NCES institutions
- Some entries may use informal names requiring alias resolution

---

### Application Platform Membership

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | Various (see platforms below) |
| **Access method** | Embedded lists in code, updated annually from organization websites |
| **File format** | Hardcoded Go data (no external download) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual — check organization websites each fall |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.admissions` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import admissions
```

**Platforms tracked:**

| Platform | Approximate Members | Source |
|----------|--------------------|--------|
| Common App | ~1,500 | https://www.commonapp.org/explore |
| Coalition (Scoir) | ~150 | https://www.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/ |
| ApplyTexas | TX public institutions | https://www.applytexas.org/ |
| Apply SUNY | SUNY system | https://www.suny.edu/ |
| UC Application | UC system (9 campuses) | https://apply.universityofcalifornia.edu/ |
| CSU Application | CSU system (23 campuses) | https://www.calstate.edu/ |

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit each platform's member list annually
2. Update the embedded lists in the importer source code
3. UC/CSU/SUNY members are detected via institution name prefix matching

---

## Common Operations

### Using the `--file` flag

All importers accept `--file` to bypass the automatic download. This is useful
when:
- The source website is down or has changed its format
- You've manually obtained data (e.g., from browser dev tools)
- You're testing with sample data

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj --file /path/to/wsj.json
```

### Cache directory

Downloaded files are cached in `~/.cache/kapow/` (configurable via
`--cache-dir` or `CACHE_DIR` env var). The fetcher uses HTTP conditional
requests (ETag / Last-Modified) to avoid re-downloading unchanged files.

Cache files are XZ-compressed with JSON sidecar metadata.

### Dry run mode

Validate parsing without writing to the database:

```bash
kapow import nces --dry-run
kapow run-all --dry-run
```

### Running everything

```bash
kapow run-all                          # all importers in dependency order
kapow run-all --only=nces,rankings     # specific importers only
kapow run-all --skip=weather,crime     # skip specific importers
```

toolUseResult.content:
# Kapow Data Sources

Comprehensive reference for every data source in the kapow data pipeline.
Each section documents the URL, access method, file format, update cadence,
fetch command, and any manual steps required.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Annual Import Schedule](#annual-import-schedule)
- [Pipeline Ordering](#pipeline-ordering)
- [Foundation Data](#foundation-data)
  - [College Scorecard (NCES/IPEDS)](#college-scorecard-ncesipeds)
  - [College Scorecard Field of Study](#college-scorecard-field-of-study)
  - [Urban Institute Education Data Portal](#urban-institute-education-data-portal)
- [Rankings](#rankings)
  - [US News & World Report](#us-news--world-report)
  - [Forbes](#forbes)
  - [Niche](#niche)
  - [Times Higher Education](#times-higher-education)
  - [QS World University Rankings](#qs-world-university-rankings)
  - [Washington Monthly](#washington-monthly)
  - [WSJ/College Pulse](#wsjcollege-pulse)
  - [ARWU / Shanghai Ranking](#arwu--shanghai-ranking)
  - [Money Magazine](#money-magazine)
- [Athletics](#athletics)
  - [NCAA Membership Directory](#ncaa-membership-directory)
- [Geographic & Environmental](#geographic--environmental)
  - [Köppen Climate Classification](#köppen-climate-classification)
  - [ZIP Code Coordinates (GeoNames)](#zip-code-coordinates-geonames)
- [Political & Demographic](#political--demographic)
  - [FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean](#fivethirtyeight-partisan-lean)
- [Campus Safety](#campus-safety)
  - [Clery Act Crime Data](#clery-act-crime-data)
- [Institutional Metadata](#institutional-metadata)
  - [University Hex Colors](#university-hex-colors)
  - [Application Platform Membership](#application-platform-membership)

---

## Annual Import Schedule

Run sources in this order each year. The `run-all` command handles dependency
ordering automatically, but this calendar shows when new data becomes available.

| Month | Sources Available | Command |
|-------|-------------------|---------|
| **May–Jun** | College Scorecard + FOS, QS World, Money | `kapow import nces && kapow import nces-fos` |
| **Aug–Sep** | ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ | `kapow import rankings --publication=<slug>` |
| **Oct–Nov** | Times Higher Education, Crime/Clery | `kapow import rankings --publication=the && kapow import crime` |
| **Any time** | Köppen, ZIP coords, partisan lean, hex colors, app platforms | `kapow run-all --only=weather,zipcoords,misc` |
| **After each batch** | Normalizer + materialized view refresh | `kapow import normalizer` (automatic in `run-all`) |

---

## Pipeline Ordering

The `run-all` command executes importers in dependency order using topological
sort:

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

---

## Foundation Data

### College Scorecard (NCES/IPEDS)

The primary institutional data source. All other importers match against
institutions established by this import.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download (ZIP archive) |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual. Late April–May (Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024). October update adds FSA data. |
| **Expected by** | Jun 30 |
| **DB target** | `search_base.institution` core columns + `nces` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import nces
kapow import nces --file /path/to/Most-Recent-Cohorts-Institution.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- Institution identity: name, unit_id, OPE6/OPE8 IDs, city, state, coordinates
- Demographics, test scores, costs, admissions rates, enrollment
- 200+ columns mapped to JSONB keys

**Special handling:**
- OPE6/OPE8 deduplication: prefers lower unit_id when multiple campuses share an OPE
- Filters out non-degree-granting institutions
- This is Layer 0 — must run before any other importer

---

### College Scorecard Field of Study

CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) data from the same Scorecard
release.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ (FieldOfStudyData files) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download (ZIP archive) |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Update cadence** | Same as College Scorecard |
| **Expected by** | Jun 30 |
| **DB target** | `search_base.cip_data` + `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import nces-fos
```

**What it imports:**
- CIP codes and program names by institution
- Credential levels: 1=Certificate, 2=Associate, 3=Bachelor's, 4=Master's, 5=Doctoral, 6=Professional
- Earnings and debt data by program

---

### Urban Institute Education Data Portal

Aggregated API that provides cleaned, standardized access to IPEDS, Scorecard,
EADA, Crime, FSA, NACUBO, NHGIS, and PSEO data.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://educationdata.urban.org/ |
| **API docs** | https://educationdata.urban.org/documentation/ |
| **Access method** | REST API (JSON) — no authentication required |
| **File format** | JSON (paginated API responses) |
| **Update cadence** | Varies by dataset; generally follows original source release dates |
| **Expected by** | N/A — data appears after Urban processes upstream releases |

**API pattern:**

```
https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/ipeds/directory/{year}/
https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/scorecard/earnings/{year}/
```

**Datasets available through this API:**
- **IPEDS** — institutional characteristics, enrollment, completions, finance
- **College Scorecard** — earnings, debt, repayment
- **EADA** — Equity in Athletics Disclosure Act data
- **Campus Safety (Clery)** — crime statistics
- **FSA** — Federal Student Aid
- **NACUBO** — endowment data
- **NHGIS** — geographic/census data
- **PSEO** — Post-Secondary Employment Outcomes

**When to use:** The Urban Institute API is useful as a supplementary or
alternative source when the raw CSV downloads from Scorecard or IPEDS are
difficult to parse, or when you need pre-joined cross-dataset queries. The
primary kapow pipeline uses direct CSV downloads for the core Scorecard import
but may use this API for supplementary datasets (EADA, NACUBO, PSEO).

---

## Rankings

All ranking importers share common infrastructure:
- **Name matching**: Tier 1 (exact/normalized/alias) → Tier 2 (Jaro-Winkler fuzzy) → Tier 3 (LLM, optional)
- **Output**: `search_base.ranking` metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB
- **Dependency**: Requires NCES import first (Layer 1)

### US News & World Report

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings |
| **Access method** | GitHub CSV download (historical archive 1984–2024) |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late September |
| **Expected by** | Oct 15 |
| **Categories** | National Universities, Liberal Arts Colleges |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=usnews
kapow import rankings --publication=usnews --file /path/to/usnews.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- Numeric ranks and tier strings (e.g., "Tier 1", "51-75")
- Two separate ranking lists: national universities and liberal arts colleges
- Historical data back to 1984

**Manual steps:** For the current year's data (before it appears in the frishberg
archive), you may need to manually obtain the CSV from US News or scrape
`usnews.com/best-colleges/rankings/national-universities`.

---

### Forbes

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000` |
| **Access method** | JSON API (public, no authentication) |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August–early September |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=forbes
kapow import rankings --publication=forbes --year 2025
```

**API URL pattern:**

```
https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=organizationName,rank,state,uri
```

**What it imports:**
- Single combined list (not split by category)
- Rank, institution name, state

---

### Niche

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/` |
| **Access method** | Paginated JSON API (requires specific headers) |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=niche
```

**API URL pattern:**

```
https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college
```

**Special handling:**
- 109 pages of results
- **Required headers**: `User-Agent` (browser-like) + `Referer: https://www.niche.com/colleges/search/best-colleges/`
- **Rate limiting**: 200ms sleep between page requests
- Responses include IPEDS IDs, enabling direct matching without name resolution

---

### Times Higher Education

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` |
| **Alternative** | https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data |
| **Access method** | JSON API (behind page) or frishberg CSV |
| **File format** | JSON or CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, early–mid October |
| **Expected by** | Nov 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=the
kapow import rankings --publication=the --file /path/to/the-rankings.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit the THE rankings page in a browser
2. Open browser dev tools → Network tab
3. Filter for XHR/JSON requests to find the API endpoint
4. Download the JSON response
5. Provide via `--file` flag

Alternatively, use the frishberg CSV archive if available for the target year.

**What it imports:**
- Numeric ranks and tier bands (e.g., "201-250", "401-500")
- Name matching via aliases for institutions with non-standard names

---

### QS World University Rankings

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data |
| **Access method** | TSV/CSV download |
| **File format** | TSV (tab-separated) or CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, mid June |
| **Expected by** | Jul 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=qs
kapow import rankings --publication=qs --file /path/to/qs-world.tsv
```

**Special handling:**
- Handles tied ranks denoted by trailing `=` (e.g., "51=")
- Multiple years may be present in a single file
- Filter to US institutions only during import

---

### Washington Monthly

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://washingtonmonthly.com/rankings/` |
| **Access method** | JSON or web scrape |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late August (published with Sep/Oct issue) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 15 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=washmonthly
```

**What it imports:**
- Category-based rankings: National Universities, Liberal Arts Colleges
- Matching by unit_id when available, name matching as fallback

**Manual steps:** Check current website structure — the data format may change
year to year. If automated download fails, use browser dev tools to capture the
JSON payload and provide via `--file`.

---

### WSJ/College Pulse

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | WSJ rankings page |
| **Access method** | JSON embedded in page source |
| **File format** | JSON (`{ colleges: [...] }` structure) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, early September |
| **Expected by** | Oct 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj --file /path/to/wsj.json
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit the WSJ college rankings page
2. View page source or use browser dev tools
3. Search for the JSON object containing `colleges` array
4. Save the JSON to a file
5. Import with `--file` flag

**What it imports:**
- Direct unit_id matching where available
- Name fallback for unmatched institutions

---

### ARWU / Shanghai Ranking

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://www.shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` |
| **Access method** | HTML scrape or PDF download |
| **File format** | HTML table or PDF |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, mid August (consistently since 2003) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 1 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=arwu
kapow import rankings --publication=arwu --file /path/to/arwu.json
```

**Manual steps (if automated scrape fails):**
1. Visit `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}`
2. Option A: Use goquery to scrape the HTML table
3. Option B: Download PDF from `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`
4. Extract institution names and ranks into JSON/CSV
5. Import with `--file` flag

**What it imports:**
- Global ranks (top 500–1000 institutions)
- Filter to US institutions during import

---

### Money Magazine

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://money.com/best-colleges/` |
| **Access method** | HTML scrape |
| **File format** | HTML table with embedded data |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, late June |
| **Expected by** | Jul 31 |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=money
kapow import rankings --publication=money --file /path/to/money.json
```

**Manual steps (if automated scrape fails):**
1. Visit `money.com/best-colleges/`
2. Extract `tr[data-id]` rows from the HTML table
3. Parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` elements via goquery
4. Save as JSON/CSV and import with `--file`

---

## Athletics

### NCAA Membership Directory

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | `https://web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` |
| **Access method** | CSV export or Playwright browser automation |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Annual, August (academic year start) |
| **Expected by** | Sep 30 |
| **DB target** | `institution.athletics` JSONB + `search_base.conference` |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import ncaa
kapow import ncaa --file /path/to/ncaa-directory.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit https://web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList
2. Export the full member list as CSV (or use Playwright-based scraper)
3. Import with `--file` flag if manual export

**What it imports:**
- Division (I, II, III) and subdivision (FBS, FCS for football)
- Conference membership
- 38 sports mapped to gender categories (men/women/coed)
- Institution name matching via Tier 1/2/3

---

## Geographic & Environmental

### Köppen Climate Classification

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | Public domain ASCII grid (Kottek et al. or Beck et al.) |
| **Access method** | Direct download |
| **File format** | ASCII text (space-delimited grid: lat, lon, classification) |
| **Update cadence** | Stable — updated approximately every 10 years |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.weather` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import weather
kapow import weather --file /path/to/koppen.txt
```

**What it imports:**
- Köppen-Geiger climate classification code for each institution's coordinates
- E.g., Cfa (humid subtropical), Dfb (warm-summer humid continental)

**Special handling:**
- Grid-based lookup: normalizes coordinates to 0.25°/0.75° grid
- Spiral search up to 3° for coastal institutions where the nearest grid cell is ocean
- Requires institution coordinates from NCES import

---

### ZIP Code Coordinates (GeoNames)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | GeoNames postal code data (public domain) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download |
| **File format** | CSV (ZIP code, latitude, longitude, municipality, state) |
| **Update cadence** | Stable — updated continuously but rarely changes |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `location.zip_coordinates` (PostGIS geography) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import zipcoords
kapow import zipcoords --file /path/to/zipcodes.csv
```

**What it imports:**
- ~42,000 US ZIP codes with latitude/longitude
- PostGIS geography points for spatial queries

**Special handling:**
- Layer 0 importer (no dependencies)
- Bulk insert via `COPY FROM` for performance

---

## Political & Demographic

### FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | FiveThirtyEight GitHub repository (CSV) |
| **Access method** | Direct CSV download from GitHub |
| **File format** | CSV |
| **Update cadence** | Post-election, every 2 years (typically March in odd years) |
| **Expected by** | Mar 31 (odd years, e.g., 2027, 2029) |
| **DB target** | `institution.misc` JSONB (`partisan_lean` key) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import misc --source=partisan
```

**What it imports:**
- Congressional-district-level partisan lean scores (D+/R+ values)
- Matched to institutions via PostGIS spatial join to congressional district boundaries

**Special handling:**
- Requires institution coordinates from NCES import
- Requires congressional district geometries in the database
- Score represents the district where the institution is physically located

---

## Campus Safety

### Clery Act Crime Data

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list |
| **Access method** | Direct download (XLS files, converted to CSV) |
| **File format** | XLS → CSV (requires LibreOffice for conversion) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual. Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **Expected by** | Nov 15 |
| **DB target** | `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import crime
kapow import crime --file /path/to/crime-oncampus.csv
```

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list
2. Download the XLS files for the target year
3. Convert to CSV using LibreOffice:
   ```bash
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv oncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv noncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv vawa-oncampus.xls
   libreoffice --headless --convert-to csv vawa-noncampus.xls
   ```
4. Import the converted CSVs

**What it imports:**
- On-campus crime statistics (murder, robbery, burglary, etc.)
- Non-campus crime statistics
- VAWA categories (domestic violence, dating violence, stalking)
- Deduplicates by OPE8 ID (aggregates across branch campuses)

**Files required (4 CSVs per year):**

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `oncampus.csv` | On-campus crime counts |
| `noncampus.csv` | Non-campus crime counts |
| `vawa-oncampus.csv` | VAWA on-campus incidents |
| `vawa-noncampus.csv` | VAWA non-campus incidents |

---

## Institutional Metadata

### University Hex Colors

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | https://github.com/frishberg/University-Hex-Colors |
| **Access method** | GitHub JSON download |
| **File format** | JSON |
| **Update cadence** | Community-maintained; check periodically |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.misc` JSONB (`hex_color` key) |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import misc --source=hexcolors
kapow import misc --source=hexcolors --file /path/to/colors.json
```

**What it imports:**
- Primary and secondary hex color codes for universities
- Used for branding in the frontend UI

**Special handling:**
- Name matching via Tier 1/2 to link color entries to NCES institutions
- Some entries may use informal names requiring alias resolution

---

### Application Platform Membership

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **URL** | Various (see platforms below) |
| **Access method** | Embedded lists in code, updated annually from organization websites |
| **File format** | Hardcoded Go data (no external download) |
| **Update cadence** | Annual — check organization websites each fall |
| **Expected by** | N/A |
| **DB target** | `institution.admissions` JSONB |

**Fetch command:**

```bash
kapow import admissions
```

**Platforms tracked:**

| Platform | Approximate Members | Source |
|----------|--------------------|--------|
| Common App | ~1,500 | https://www.commonapp.org/explore |
| Coalition (Scoir) | ~150 | https://www.coalitionforcollegeaccess.org/ |
| ApplyTexas | TX public institutions | https://www.applytexas.org/ |
| Apply SUNY | SUNY system | https://www.suny.edu/ |
| UC Application | UC system (9 campuses) | https://apply.universityofcalifornia.edu/ |
| CSU Application | CSU system (23 campuses) | https://www.calstate.edu/ |

**Manual steps:**
1. Visit each platform's member list annually
2. Update the embedded lists in the importer source code
3. UC/CSU/SUNY members are detected via institution name prefix matching

---

## Common Operations

### Using the `--file` flag

All importers accept `--file` to bypass the automatic download. This is useful
when:
- The source website is down or has changed its format
- You've manually obtained data (e.g., from browser dev tools)
- You're testing with sample data

```bash
kapow import rankings --publication=wsj --file /path/to/wsj.json
```

### Cache directory

Downloaded files are cached in `~/.cache/kapow/` (configurable via
`--cache-dir` or `CACHE_DIR` env var). The fetcher uses HTTP conditional
requests (ETag / Last-Modified) to avoid re-downloading unchanged files.

Cache files are XZ-compressed with JSON sidecar metadata.

### Dry run mode

Validate parsing without writing to the database:

```bash
kapow import nces --dry-run
kapow run-all --dry-run
```

### Running everything

```bash
kapow run-all                          # all importers in dependency order
kapow run-all --only=nces,rankings     # specific importers only
kapow run-all --skip=weather,crime     # skip specific importers
```

- **Foundation**: College Scorecard, Scorecard FOS, Urban Institute API
- **Rankings** (9): US News, Forbes, Niche, THE, QS, Washington Monthly, WSJ, ARWU, Money
- **Athletics**: NCAA Membership Directory
- **Geographic**: Köppen climate, ZIP coordinates (GeoNames)
